$comment: generated by the ocm schema generation tool
title: Config
description:
Config is the canonical wire format for transfer settings. It is carried as an
entry inside the central generic configuration
(generic.config.ocm.software/v1) and extracted with [LookupConfig].
Downstream consumers (CLI, controllers) pass the result into
[transfer.FromConfig], so any new transfer setting belongs here first.

type: generic.config.ocm.software/v1
configurations:
- type: transfer.config.ocm.software/v1alpha1
recursive: -1
copyMode: localBlob

copyMode.description:
CopyMode determines which resources are copied during a transfer operation.

When building a transformation graph, the CopyMode controls whether only local blob
resources are included or all resources (including remote OCI artifacts and Helm charts)
are fetched and re-uploaded to the target repository.

recursive.description:
Recursive configures transferring component references with the parent component.
It accepts either an integer or a boolean: -1 means infinite recursion, 0 means
no recursion, n > 0 limits recursion to n levels; true is shorthand for -1 and
false for 0. See [Recursive].

uploadType.description:
UploadType determines how resources are stored in the target repository during transfer.

This option is only relevant when resources are being copied (i.e., when [CopyModeAllResources]
is set or for local blob resources in the default mode). It controls whether resources are
embedded as local blobs within the component descriptor or uploaded as separate OCI artifacts
with their own repository references.

ocm.software.open-component-model.bindin.$comment: this core runtime schema was automatically included by the ocm schema generation tool to allow introspection
ocm.software.open-component-model.bindin.title: Type
ocm.software.open-component-model.bindin.description: Type represents a structured type with an optional version and a name. It is used to identify the type of an object in a versioned API.

ocm.software.open-component-model.bindin.$comment: generated by the ocm schema generation tool
ocm.software.open-component-model.bindin.title: CopyMode
ocm.software.open-component-model.bindin.description:
CopyMode determines which resources are copied during a transfer operation.

When building a transformation graph, the CopyMode controls whether only local blob
resources are included or all resources (including remote OCI artifacts and Helm charts)
are fetched and re-uploaded to the target repository.

oneOf.description:
CopyModeLocalBlobResources is the default copy mode. It transfers only resources
that are stored as local blobs within the source repository. Remote references
(such as OCI image artifacts or Helm charts hosted externally) are left as-is
in the component descriptor - their access specifications are preserved unchanged.

CopyModeAllResources transfers all resources regardless of their access type.
Remote OCI artifacts are downloaded and re-uploaded to the target, and Helm charts
are fetched, converted to OCI format, and stored in the target repository.
This mode ensures the target repository is fully self-contained.

ocm.software.open-component-model.bindin.$comment: Recursive is a Go int that also accepts a boolean in the wire format: true means infinite recursion (-1) and false means no recursion (0). It always marshals back out as an integer.
ocm.software.open-component-model.bindin.title: Recursive
ocm.software.open-component-model.bindin.description:
Recursive controls whether component references are transferred along with
their parent component, and how deep that recursion goes.

anyOf.description: Recursion depth: -1 means infinite recursion, 0 means no recursion, n > 0 limits recursion to n levels.
Shorthand: true maps to infinite recursion (-1), false to no recursion (0).

ocm.software.open-component-model.bindin.$comment: generated by the ocm schema generation tool
ocm.software.open-component-model.bindin.title: UploadType
ocm.software.open-component-model.bindin.description:
UploadType determines how resources are stored in the target repository during transfer.

This option is only relevant when resources are being copied (i.e., when [CopyModeAllResources]
is set or for local blob resources in the default mode). It controls whether resources are
embedded as local blobs within the component descriptor or uploaded as separate OCI artifacts
with their own repository references.

oneOf.description:
UploadAsDefault lets the transfer logic decide the upload strategy based on the source
access type and target repository capabilities. Local blob resources remain as local blobs,
and the original access semantics are preserved where possible.

UploadAsLocalBlob forces all transferred resources to be stored as local blobs
in the target repository. The resource content is embedded directly in the
component version's OCI manifest layers.

UploadAsOciArtifact uploads transferred resources as separate OCI artifacts in the
target registry, each with their own repository and tag. The component descriptor's
resource access is updated to reference the new OCI image location. This is only
supported when the target is an OCI registry (not CTF).
